values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

Please don't add more code to libcfs. Since this is only used by llite we can merge this work there. These abstraction layers give the upstream guys heart burn.

Patch Set 4:

This is only first place I use it. This is general code which will be used in many places for parallelization. For example, I'm going to use it for parallel checksum calculation.

Patch Set 11: Code-Review-1

Please put this is in the clio layer.

Patch Set 11:

James,

I still disagree.  This is a general purpose parallel task framework.  Why should it go in the CLIO layer?

Patch Set 11: Code-Review-1

(20 comments)

While I agree that this is mostly a generic infrastructure that should be in libcfs and usable from multiple parts of the code, the current implementation intertwines the generic part with CLIO-specific implementation details (e.g. the engine types, I/O chunk sizes, etc).

I think with a small amount of restructuring that this can be fixed and make the code a lot cleaner.

Definitely getting better, but I think the current implementation has a problem with the way the engines are assigned.

Patch Set 13: Code-Review-1

Please don't add more junk to libcfs.

Patch Set 13:

James, Where you propose to place this code? Create new module with common code in the same way as libcfs? This API can be used in lnet, clio and other part of lustre. I cannot place it in llite because of I cannot use it from lnet in this case. Potentially this code can replace the functionality of workitem.c from libcfs. Can you look to this code as replacement for workitem.c from libcfs?

Patch Set 13:

James, I don't think comments like that are productive.

I think this code is well suited to be in libcfs, since it is generic enough to be usable in different parts of the code, even if it is currently only used for readahead.

Patch Set 14:

Sorry didn't mean to sound harsh. Just trying to avoid any further expansion of libcfs since we have been moving to shrink it to only be a distro compatibility module. I was originally thinking having this work be apart of the obdclass module. 

Now if you plan to use this to replace the workitem then I have no problem with this in the libcfs layer. Also if we sell it as a replacement for workitem upstream will be less likely to push back. Dmitry can you create a add on patch that replaces the workitem with this as well? Thanks.

Patch Set 14:

Oh can you place that in the commit message that this is intended to replace workitem.

parents.subject: LU-9513 liblustre: always check return value

commit.subject: LU-8964 libcfs: Introduce parallel tasks framework
commit.message:
LU-8964 libcfs: Introduce parallel tasks framework

In this patch new API for parallel tasks execution is introduced.
This API based on Linux kernel padata API which is used to perform
encryption and decryption on large numbers of packets without
reordering those packets.

It was adopted for general use in Lustre for parallelization of
various functionality. The first place of its usage is parallel I/O
implementation.

The first step in using it is to set up a cfs_ptask structure to
control of how this task are to be run:

    #include <libcfs/libcfs_ptask.h>

    int cfs_ptask_init(struct cfs_ptask *ptask, cfs_ptask_cb_t cbfunc,
                       void *cbdata, unsigned int flags, int cpu);

The cbfunc function with cbdata argument will be called in the process
of getting the task done. The cpu specifies which CPU will be used for
the final callback when the task is done.

The submission of task is done with:

    int cfs_ptask_submit(struct cfs_ptask *ptask,
                         struct cfs_ptask_engine *engine);

The task is submitted to the engine for execution.

In order to wait for result of task execution you should call:

    int cfs_ptask_wait_for(struct cfs_ptask *ptask);

The tasks with flag PTF_ORDERED are executed in parallel but complete
into submission order. So, waiting for last ordered task you can be sure
that all previous tasks were done before this task complete.

Test-Parameters: trivial
Change-Id: I36d9802226f9b6fae627594a86ad1a83f7bc6b0c
Signed-off-by: Dmitry Eremin <dmitry.eremin@intel.com>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/24474
Reviewed-by: Jinshan Xiong <jinshan.xiong@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
